mission: PG migration landscape analysis — full migration story

repository_state.note: ALL PG migration work is UNCOMMITTED. Committed codebase is SQLite-only.

pg_migrations.contents_summary: Initial PG schema — CREATE TABLE for: account_state, account, control_account, workspace, data_migration, project, message, part, permission, session_message, session, todo, session_share, event_sequence, event. ~177 lines of DDL.

CREATE TABLE runtime_events (id, session_id, run_id, parent_event_id, correlation_id, ts, actor, event_type, phase, status, tool_name, file_path, model, duration_ms, token_input, token_output, error_code, error_message, recoverable, payload_json, campaign_id, lane_id, role). 8 indexes.
CREATE TABLE coordination_claim, coordination_fan_out (composite PK: session_id+wave+wave_type), coordination_reservation. Coordination tables for cross-session task management.
ALTER TABLE coordination_fan_out ALTER COLUMN task_ids SET DATA TYPE jsonb — corrective migration for JSONB column type.

sqlite_migrations.note: These 20 SQLite migration directories still exist in committed git history but have been deleted from the working tree by agent sessions. The deletion is uncommitted.

naming_convention.structure: Each migration is a directory containing migration.sql + snapshot.json
naming_convention.ordering: Chronological by timestamp — no manual version numbers

console_migrations.note: PlanetScale/MySQL migrations for the console app — unrelated to opencode's PG migration

stats_migrations.note: PlanetScale/MySQL migrations for the stats app — unrelated to opencode's PG migration

definition.signature: export async function applyMigrations(db: PgClient): Promise<void>
definition.full_body_summary: Dual-path migration runner: (1) PGlite path — reads migration files via readMigrationFiles() and executes each SQL statement via client.exec(); (2) node-postgres path — uses drizzle-orm's built-in migrate(). Duck-type detection via typeof client.exec === 'function'.

importers.import: import { init as initPg, applyMigrations } from "#db"
import { init as initPg, applyMigrations } from "#db"
importers.purpose: DB adapter — auto-apply on remote PG

callers_runtime.context: Inside makePgAdapter() — remote PG only. Fire-and-forget, non-fatal. COMMENT: 'Auto-apply pending DB migrations on first connect, so fresh installs have their schema ready before any queries.'
Inside runMigrations() — CLI command. Wrapped in try/finally with pool cleanup. Called when running 'bun run db:migrate'.

connection_factories.description: Dual-path connection factory — overloaded. Accepts connectionString + ssl OR InitOptions.

branches.code: new Pool({connectionString, ssl, max, min, ...}) → drizzlePg({ client: pool })

connection_factories.description: Bridge from PgPool (managed lifecycle) to Drizzle ORM client.
Lazy singleton wrapper. Calls init(dbPath) where dbPath is resolved from getPath(). @deprecated — bypasses adapter layer. Used by makeLocalPgAdapter().
connection_factories.db_path_resolution: getPath() checks Flag.OPENCODE_DB → falls back to getChannelPath() → path.join(Global.Path.data, 'opencode.db')

primary_layer.name: DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer
primary_layer.decision_logic: If resolvedUrl(config) !== undefined → PgAdapter({connectionString, ssl, poolSize}). Else → LocalPgAdapter.

local_pg_layer.name: LocalPgAdapter
local_pg_layer.note: DOES NOT call applyMigrations — this is the critical gap

remote_pg_layer.name: PgAdapter(options)
remote_pg_layer.features: Layer-scoped lifecycle, retry schedules, HealthRegistry registration, auto-apply migrations on first connect

env_vars.OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL: Config.redacted — Remote PG connection string. If not set, falls back to local PGlite.

cli_effect.note: Optionally runs InstanceBootstrap (config + plugin init) and InstanceStore disposal

desktop_electron.note: Spawns sidecar utilityProcess, handles migration-progress events

layer_provision_chain.description: AppRuntime (app-runtime.ts:132) manages the full Effect runtime
BootstrapRuntime (bootstrap-runtime.ts:31) is a lighter runtime for bootstrapping
InstanceLayer (instance-layer.ts:4) uses Layer.unwrap to lazy-load InstanceBootstrap
layer_provision_chain.pattern: Layer.unwrap(Effect.promise(async () => { const { InstanceBootstrap } = await import('./bootstrap'); return InstanceStore.defaultLayer.pipe(Layer.provide(InstanceBootstrap.defaultLayer)) }))

layer_provision_chain.decision: Check resolvedUrl(DatabaseConfig.Service) — if set → PgAdapter (remote PG with migrations); else → LocalPgAdapter (local PGlite WITHOUT migrations)

6_startup_flow.full_runtime_graph: App starts → AppRuntime/BootstrapRuntime → AppLayer/BootstrapLayer → DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer → Layer.unwrap(config check) → PgAdapter(Layer.scoped, retry, health, MIGRATIONS) OR LocalPgAdapter(Layer.effect, sync, NO MIGRATIONS) → 60+ services use yield* DatabaseAdapter.Service for DB access

7_why_applyMigrations_never_called.root_cause: makeLocalPgAdapter() at adapter.ts:86 does NOT call applyMigrations(). Only makePgAdapter() at adapter.ts:185 calls it.

evidence.fact: makeLocalPgAdapter() creates an Interface using Database.use() (the legacy db.ts Client() singleton) which calls db.pg.ts:init() to create a PGlite client. It does NOT call applyMigrations().
makePgAdapter() calls initPg(options) then immediately calls applyMigrations(client).catch(...) as a fire-and-forget auto-apply.

The DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer at adapter.ts:393 uses Layer.unwrap() to check DatabaseConfig. If OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is NOT set (the default for local dev), it returns LocalPgAdapter — which never applies migrations.
The CLI migrate.ts script calls applyMigrations() directly, but this is a manual command ('bun run db:migrate') — not automatic at startup.
The desktop sidecar's node.ts re-exports applyMigrations but does not call it — the sidecar previously used JsonMigration (SQLite) and a comment was added to replace it.

7_why_applyMigrations_never_called.impact: On a fresh local install (no OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL set, no existing database file), the PGlite database is created but migrations are never applied. Tables do not exist. Any query will fail. This is a confirmed pre-existing bug recorded in: docs/json/opencode/knowledge/findings.v1.jsonl (lines 51, 70) and docs/json/opencode/bug-hunt-report.v1.json (line 13).
7_why_applyMigrations_never_called.fix_location: Add applyMigrations(client).catch(...) inside makeLocalPgAdapter() at adapter.ts:86, before returning the Service. Or add it to the LocalPgAdapter Layer at line 155.

pre_existing_bug_reports.finding: applyMigrations defined at db.pg.ts:25 but never called anywhere in runtime
applyMigrations() never called at startup — DB schema missing on fresh launch
Local PGlite does not auto-apply migrations at startup — makeLocalPgAdapter() never calls applyMigrations()
applyMigrations never called — listed as notable pre-existing bug
